医疗数据集.title: MosaicMRI: A Diverse Dataset and Benchmark for Raw Musculoskeletal MRI
医疗数据集.summary: Deep learning underpins a wide range of applications in MRI, including reconstruction, artifact removal, and segmentation. However, progress has been driven largely by public datasets focused on brain and knee imaging, shaping how models are trained and evaluated. As a result, careful studies of the reliability of these models across diverse anatomical settings remain limited. In this work, we introduce MosaicMRI, a large and diverse collection of fully sampled raw musculoskeletal (MSK) MR measu
医疗数据集.abstract: Deep learning underpins a wide range of applications in MRI, including reconstruction, artifact removal, and segmentation. However, progress has been driven largely by public datasets focused on brain and knee imaging, shaping how models are trained and evaluated. As a result, careful studies of the reliability of these models across diverse anatomical settings remain limited. In this work, we introduce MosaicMRI, a large and diverse collection of fully sampled raw musculoskeletal (MSK) MR measurements designed for training and evaluating machine-learning-based methods. MosaicMRI is the largest open-source raw MSK MRI dataset to date, comprising 2,671 volumes and 80,156 slices. The dataset offers substantial diversity in volume orientation (e.g., axial, sagittal), imaging contrasts (e.g., PD, T1, T2), anatomies (e.g., spine, knee, hip, ankle, and others), and numbers of acquisition coils. Using VarNet as a baseline for accelerated reconstruction task, we perform a comprehensive set of experiments to study scaling behavior with respect to both model capacity and dataset size. Interestingly, models trained on the combined anatomies significantly outperform anatomy-specific models in low-sample regimes, highlighting the benefits of anatomical diversity and the presence of exploitable cross-anatomical correlations. We further evaluate robustness and cross-anatomy generalization by training models on one anatomy (e.g., spine) and testing them on another (e.g., knee). Notably, we identify groups of body parts (e.g., foot and elbow) that generalize well with each other, and highlight that performance under domain shifts depends on both training set size, anatomy, and protocol-specific factors.
医疗数据集.llm_keywords: 肌肉骨骼MRI, 深度学习, 数据集, 加速重建, 跨解剖泛化
医疗数据集.llm_summary: 本文介绍了MosaicMRI，一个大规模、多样化的开源原始肌肉骨骼MRI数据集，用于训练和评估基于机器学习的方法。通过实验发现，在低样本情况下，多解剖结构联合训练的模型优于单一解剖结构模型，并探索了模型在不同解剖结构间的泛化能力。
医疗数据集.abstract_cn: 深度学习支撑着MRI中的广泛应用，包括重建、伪影去除和分割。然而，进展主要由专注于大脑和膝盖成像的公共数据集驱动，这塑造了模型的训练和评估方式。因此，对这些模型在不同解剖环境下的可靠性的仔细研究仍然有限。在这项工作中，我们介绍了MosaicMRI，一个大规模且多样化的全采样原始肌肉骨骼（MSK）MR测量值集合，专为训练和评估基于机器学习的方法而设计。MosaicMRI是迄今为止最大的开源原始MSK MRI数据集，包含2,671个体积和80,156个切片。该数据集在体积方向（例如，轴向、矢状面）、成像对比度（例如，PD、T1、T2）、解剖结构（例如，脊柱、膝盖、髋部、脚踝等）以及采集线圈数量方面提供了显著的多样性。使用VarNet作为加速重建任务的基线，我们进行了一系列全面的实验，以研究模型容量和数据集大小方面的缩放行为。有趣的是，在低样本情况下，在组合解剖结构上训练的模型显著优于特定解剖结构模型，突显了解剖多样性的好处以及可利用的跨解剖相关性。我们进一步通过在一个解剖结构（例如，脊柱）上训练模型并在另一个（例如，膝盖）上测试它们来评估鲁棒性和跨解剖泛化能力。值得注意的是，我们识别了彼此泛化良好的身体部位组（例如，脚和肘部），并强调域转移下的性能取决于训练集大小、解剖结构和协议特定因素。

医疗数据集.title: Towards Brain MRI Foundation Models for the Clinic: Findings from the FOMO25 Challenge
医疗数据集.summary: Clinical deployment of automated brain MRI analysis faces a fundamental challenge: clinical data is heterogeneous and noisy, and high-quality labels are prohibitively costly to obtain. Self-supervised learning (SSL) can address this by leveraging the vast amounts of unlabeled data produced in clinical workflows to train robust \textit{foundation models} that adapt out-of-domain with minimal supervision. However, the development of foundation models for brain MRI has been limited by small pretrai
医疗数据集.abstract: Clinical deployment of automated brain MRI analysis faces a fundamental challenge: clinical data is heterogeneous and noisy, and high-quality labels are prohibitively costly to obtain. Self-supervised learning (SSL) can address this by leveraging the vast amounts of unlabeled data produced in clinical workflows to train robust \textit{foundation models} that adapt out-of-domain with minimal supervision. However, the development of foundation models for brain MRI has been limited by small pretraining datasets and in-domain benchmarking focused on high-quality, research-grade data. To address this gap, we organized the FOMO25 challenge as a satellite event at MICCAI 2025. FOMO25 provided participants with a large pretraining dataset, FOMO60K, and evaluated models on data sourced directly from clinical workflows in few-shot and out-of-domain settings. Tasks covered infarct classification, meningioma segmentation, and brain age regression, and considered both models trained on FOMO60K (method track) and any data (open track). Nineteen foundation models from sixteen teams were evaluated using a standardized containerized pipeline. Results show that (a) self-supervised pretraining improves generalization on clinical data under domain shift, with the strongest models trained \textit{out-of-domain} surpassing supervised baselines trained \textit{in-domain}. (b) No single pretraining objective benefits all tasks: MAE favors segmentation, hybrid reconstruction-contrastive objectives favor classification, and (c) strong performance was achieved by small pretrained models, and improvements from scaling model size and training duration did not yield reliable benefits.
医疗数据集.llm_keywords: 脑MRI基础模型, 自监督学习, 临床数据, 领域适应, 医学图像分析
医疗数据集.llm_summary: FOMO25挑战赛旨在推动脑MRI基础模型的临床部署。研究通过大规模无标签临床数据预训练模型，并在少样本和跨域任务中评估。结果表明，自监督预训练能提升模型在临床数据上的泛化能力，不同预训练目标适用于不同任务，且小型模型已能取得优异性能。
医疗数据集.abstract_cn: 自动化脑MRI分析的临床部署面临一个根本性挑战：临床数据是异构且嘈杂的，高质量标签的获取成本极高。自监督学习（SSL）可以通过利用临床工作流程中产生的大量未标记数据来训练鲁棒的	extit{基础模型}，从而以最少的监督实现跨领域适应。然而，脑MRI基础模型的发展一直受到预训练数据集小以及专注于高质量研究级数据的领域内基准测试的限制。为了弥补这一差距，我们在MICCAI 2025上组织了一场卫星活动——FOMO25挑战赛。FOMO25为参与者提供了一个大型预训练数据集FOMO60K，并在少样本和跨领域设置下，使用直接来自临床工作流程的数据评估模型。任务涵盖梗死分类、脑膜瘤分割和脑年龄回归，并考虑了在FOMO60K上训练的模型（方法赛道）和在任何数据上训练的模型（开放赛道）。使用标准化的容器化流水线评估了来自16个团队的19个基础模型。结果表明：（a）自监督预训练提高了模型在领域偏移下对临床数据的泛化能力，最强的	extit{跨领域}训练模型超过了	extit{领域内}训练的监督基线。（b）没有单一的预训练目标对所有任务都有益：MAE（掩码自编码器）有利于分割，混合重建-对比目标有利于分类。（c）小型预训练模型取得了强劲的性能，而扩大模型规模和训练时长所带来的改进并未产生可靠的收益。

医疗数据集.title: GazeVaLM: A Multi-Observer Eye-Tracking Benchmark for Evaluating Clinical Realism in AI-Generated X-Rays
医疗数据集.summary: We introduce GazeVaLM, a public eye-tracking dataset for studying clinical perception during chest radiograph authenticity assessment. The dataset comprises 960 gaze recordings from 16 expert radiologists interpreting 30 real and 30 synthetic chest X-rays (generated by diffusion based generative AI) under two conditions: diagnostic assessment and real-fake classification (Visual Turing test). For each image-observer pair, we provide raw gaze samples, fixation maps, scanpaths, saliency density ma
医疗数据集.abstract: We introduce GazeVaLM, a public eye-tracking dataset for studying clinical perception during chest radiograph authenticity assessment. The dataset comprises 960 gaze recordings from 16 expert radiologists interpreting 30 real and 30 synthetic chest X-rays (generated by diffusion based generative AI) under two conditions: diagnostic assessment and real-fake classification (Visual Turing test). For each image-observer pair, we provide raw gaze samples, fixation maps, scanpaths, saliency density maps, structured diagnostic labels, and authenticity judgments. We extend the protocol to 6 state-of-the-art multimodal LLMs, releasing their predicted diagnoses, authenticity labels, and confidence scores under matched conditions - enabling direct human-AI comparison at both decision and uncertainty levels. We further provide analyses of gaze agreement, inter-observer consistency, and benchmarking of radiologists versus LLMs in diagnostic accuracy and authenticity detection. GazeVaLM supports research in gaze modeling, clinical decision-making, human-AI comparison, generative image realism assessment, and uncertainty quantification. By jointly releasing visual attention data, clinical labels, and model predictions, we aim to facilitate reproducible research on how experts and AI systems perceive, interpret, and evaluate medical images. The dataset is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/davidcwong/GazeVaLM.
医疗数据集.llm_keywords: 眼动追踪, 人工智能生成X射线, 临床感知, 多模态大语言模型, 人机对比
医疗数据集.llm_summary: 本文介绍了GazeVaLM，一个用于评估AI生成胸部X射线临床真实性的公开眼动追踪数据集。该数据集包含16位放射科专家解读真实与合成X射线时的眼动数据，并扩展至6个多模态大语言模型，以支持人机在诊断和不确定性层面的对比研究。
医疗数据集.abstract_cn: 我们介绍了GazeVaLM，一个用于研究胸部X光片真实性评估过程中临床感知的公开眼动追踪数据集。该数据集包含来自16位放射科专家在两种条件下（诊断评估和真假分类，即视觉图灵测试）解读30张真实和30张合成（由基于扩散的生成式AI生成）胸部X射线时的960条眼动记录。对于每个图像-观察者配对，我们提供了原始注视点样本、注视点图、扫描路径、显著性密度图、结构化诊断标签和真实性判断。我们将该协议扩展到6个最先进的多模态大语言模型，发布了它们在匹配条件下的预测诊断、真实性标签和置信度分数——从而能够在决策和不确定性层面进行直接的人机比较。我们进一步提供了注视一致性、观察者间一致性的分析，以及放射科医生与大语言模型在诊断准确性和真实性检测方面的基准测试。GazeVaLM支持注视建模、临床决策、人机比较、生成图像真实性评估和不确定性量化方面的研究。通过联合发布视觉注意力数据、临床标签和模型预测，我们旨在促进关于专家和AI系统如何感知、解释和评估医学图像的可重复研究。数据集可在 https://huggingface.co/datasets/davidcwong/GazeVaLM 获取。

医疗大模型.title: Seeing Through the Tool: A Controlled Benchmark for Occlusion Robustness in Foundation Segmentation Models
医疗大模型.summary: Occlusion, where target structures are partially hidden by surgical instruments or overlapping tissues, remains a critical yet underexplored challenge for foundation segmentation models in clinical endoscopy. We introduce OccSAM-Bench, a benchmark designed to systematically evaluate SAM-family models under controlled, synthesized surgical occlusion. Our framework simulates two occlusion types (i.e., surgical tool overlay and cutout) across three calibrated severity levels on three public polyp d
医疗大模型.abstract: Occlusion, where target structures are partially hidden by surgical instruments or overlapping tissues, remains a critical yet underexplored challenge for foundation segmentation models in clinical endoscopy. We introduce OccSAM-Bench, a benchmark designed to systematically evaluate SAM-family models under controlled, synthesized surgical occlusion. Our framework simulates two occlusion types (i.e., surgical tool overlay and cutout) across three calibrated severity levels on three public polyp datasets. We propose a novel three-region evaluation protocol that decomposes segmentation performance into full, visible-only, and invisible targets. This metric exposes behaviors that standard amodal evaluation obscures, revealing two distinct model archetypes: Occluder-Aware models (SAM, SAM 2, SAM 3, MedSAM3), which prioritize visible tissue delineation and reject instruments, and Occluder-Agnostic models (MedSAM, MedSAM2), which confidently predict into occluded regions. SAM-Med2D aligns with neither and underperforms across all conditions. Ultimately, our results demonstrate that occlusion robustness is not uniform across architectures, and model selection must be driven by specific clinical intent-whether prioritizing conservative visible-tissue segmentation or the amodal inference of hidden anatomy.
医疗大模型.llm_keywords: 分割模型, 遮挡鲁棒性, 手术内窥镜, 基准测试, 模型评估
医疗大模型.llm_summary: 本文针对临床内窥镜中目标结构被手术器械或组织部分遮挡的挑战，提出了OccSAM-Bench基准，用于系统评估SAM系列模型在受控合成手术遮挡下的表现。通过三区域评估协议，研究揭示了模型在处理遮挡时的两种不同行为模式，并指出模型选择需依据具体的临床意图。
医疗大模型.abstract_cn: 遮挡（Occlusion），即目标结构被手术器械或重叠组织部分隐藏，对于临床内窥镜中的基础分割模型（foundation segmentation models）而言，仍然是一个关键但尚未被充分探索的挑战。我们引入了OccSAM-Bench，这是一个旨在受控、合成的手术遮挡下系统评估SAM系列模型（SAM-family models）的基准。我们的框架在三个公共息肉数据集上，模拟了两种遮挡类型（即手术工具覆盖和挖除）以及三个校准的严重程度。我们提出了一种新颖的三区域评估协议，将分割性能分解为完整目标、仅可见目标和不可见目标。该指标揭示了标准非模态评估（amodal evaluation）所掩盖的行为，识别出两种不同的模型原型：遮挡感知模型（Occluder-Aware models）（SAM, SAM 2, SAM 3, MedSAM3），其优先描绘可见组织并排斥器械；以及遮挡无关模型（Occluder-Agnostic models）（MedSAM, MedSAM2），其自信地预测到被遮挡区域。SAM-Med2D与两者均不一致，在所有条件下表现不佳。最终，我们的结果表明，遮挡鲁棒性（occlusion robustness）在不同架构中并不一致，模型选择必须由特定的临床意图驱动——无论是优先考虑保守的可见组织分割，还是对隐藏解剖结构的非模态推断（amodal inference）。

医疗大模型.title: Towards Brain MRI Foundation Models for the Clinic: Findings from the FOMO25 Challenge

Self-supervised learning (SSL) can address this by leveraging the vast amounts of unlabeled data produced in clinical workflows to train robust \textit{foundation models} that adapt out-of-domain with minimal supervision. However, the development of foundation models for brain MRI has been limited by small pretrai
医疗大模型.abstract: Clinical deployment of automated brain MRI analysis faces a fundamental challenge: clinical data is heterogeneous and noisy, and high-quality labels are prohibitively costly to obtain. Self-supervised learning (SSL) can address this by leveraging the vast amounts of unlabeled data produced in clinical workflows to train robust \textit{foundation models} that adapt out-of-domain with minimal supervision. However, the development of foundation models for brain MRI has been limited by small pretraining datasets and in-domain benchmarking focused on high-quality, research-grade data. To address this gap, we organized the FOMO25 challenge as a satellite event at MICCAI 2025. FOMO25 provided participants with a large pretraining dataset, FOMO60K, and evaluated models on data sourced directly from clinical workflows in few-shot and out-of-domain settings. Tasks covered infarct classification, meningioma segmentation, and brain age regression, and considered both models trained on FOMO60K (method track) and any data (open track). Nineteen foundation models from sixteen teams were evaluated using a standardized containerized pipeline. Results show that (a) self-supervised pretraining improves generalization on clinical data under domain shift, with the strongest models trained \textit{out-of-domain} surpassing supervised baselines trained \textit{in-domain}. (b) No single pretraining objective benefits all tasks: MAE favors segmentation, hybrid reconstruction-contrastive objectives favor classification, and (c) strong performance was achieved by small pretrained models, and improvements from scaling model size and training duration did not yield reliable benefits.
医疗大模型.llm_keywords: 脑MRI基础模型, 自监督学习, 临床数据, 领域适应, 医学图像分析
医疗大模型.llm_summary: FOMO25挑战赛旨在推动脑MRI基础模型的临床部署。研究通过大规模无标签临床数据预训练模型，并在少样本和跨域任务中评估。结果表明，自监督预训练能提升模型在临床数据上的泛化能力，不同预训练目标适用于不同任务，且小型模型已能取得优异性能。
医疗大模型.abstract_cn: 自动化脑MRI分析的临床部署面临一个根本性挑战：临床数据是异构且嘈杂的，高质量标签的获取成本极高。自监督学习（SSL）可以通过利用临床工作流程中产生的大量未标记数据来训练鲁棒的	extit{基础模型}，从而以最少的监督实现跨领域适应。然而，脑MRI基础模型的发展一直受到预训练数据集小以及专注于高质量研究级数据的领域内基准测试的限制。为了弥补这一差距，我们在MICCAI 2025上组织了一场卫星活动——FOMO25挑战赛。FOMO25为参与者提供了一个大型预训练数据集FOMO60K，并在少样本和跨领域设置下，使用直接来自临床工作流程的数据评估模型。任务涵盖梗死分类、脑膜瘤分割和脑年龄回归，并考虑了在FOMO60K上训练的模型（方法赛道）和在任何数据上训练的模型（开放赛道）。使用标准化的容器化流水线评估了来自16个团队的19个基础模型。结果表明：（a）自监督预训练提高了模型在领域偏移下对临床数据的泛化能力，最强的	extit{跨领域}训练模型超过了	extit{领域内}训练的监督基线。（b）没有单一的预训练目标对所有任务都有益：MAE（掩码自编码器）有利于分割，混合重建-对比目标有利于分类。（c）小型预训练模型取得了强劲的性能，而扩大模型规模和训练时长所带来的改进并未产生可靠的收益。

医疗大模型.title: Towards Autonomous Mechanistic Reasoning in Virtual Cells
医疗大模型.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have recently gained significant attention as a promising approach to accelerate scientific discovery. However, their application in open-ended scientific domains such as biology remains limited, primarily due to the lack of factually grounded and actionable explanations. To address this, we introduce a structured explanation formalism for virtual cells that represents biological reasoning as mechanistic action graphs, enabling systematic verification and falsificati
医疗大模型.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have recently gained significant attention as a promising approach to accelerate scientific discovery. However, their application in open-ended scientific domains such as biology remains limited, primarily due to the lack of factually grounded and actionable explanations. To address this, we introduce a structured explanation formalism for virtual cells that represents biological reasoning as mechanistic action graphs, enabling systematic verification and falsification. Building upon this, we propose VCR-Agent, a multi-agent framework that integrates biologically grounded knowledge retrieval with a verifier-based filtering approach to generate and validate mechanistic reasoning autonomously. Using this framework, we release VC-TRACES dataset, which consists of verified mechanistic explanations derived from the Tahoe-100M atlas. Empirically, we demonstrate that training with these explanations improves factual precision and provides a more effective supervision signal for downstream gene expression prediction. These results underscore the importance of reliable mechanistic reasoning for virtual cells, achieved through the synergy of multi-agent and rigorous verification.
医疗大模型.llm_keywords: 大语言模型, 虚拟细胞, 机制推理, 多智能体框架, 生物知识检索
医疗大模型.llm_summary: 本文针对大语言模型在生物学等开放科学领域应用受限的问题，提出了一种用于虚拟细胞的结构化解释形式，将生物推理表示为机制行动图。基于此，作者提出了VCR-Agent多智能体框架，该框架整合了基于生物知识的检索和基于验证器的过滤方法，以自主生成和验证机制推理。研究还发布了VC-TRACES数据集，并证明使用这些经过验证的解释进行训练能提高事实准确性，并为下游基因表达预测提供更有效的监督信号。
医疗大模型.abstract_cn: 大语言模型（LLMs）作为加速科学发现的一种有前景的方法，最近受到了极大关注。然而，它们在生物学等开放科学领域的应用仍然有限，这主要是由于缺乏基于事实且可操作的解释。为了解决这个问题，我们为虚拟细胞引入了一种结构化的解释形式，将生物推理表示为机制行动图（mechanistic action graphs），从而支持系统性的验证和证伪。在此基础上，我们提出了VCR-Agent，这是一个多智能体框架（multi-agent framework），它整合了基于生物知识的检索（biologically grounded knowledge retrieval）和基于验证器的过滤方法（verifier-based filtering approach），以自主生成和验证机制推理。利用该框架，我们发布了VC-TRACES数据集，该数据集包含从Tahoe-100M图谱中提取的经过验证的机制解释。实验表明，使用这些解释进行训练可以提高事实精确度，并为下游基因表达预测（gene expression prediction）提供更有效的监督信号。这些结果强调了通过多智能体协同和严格验证来实现虚拟细胞可靠机制推理的重要性。

医疗大模型.title: Exploring Radiologists' Expectations of Explainable Machine Learning Models in Medical Image Analysis
医疗大模型.summary: In spite of the strong performance of machine learning (ML) models in radiology, they have not been widely accepted by radiologists, limiting clinical integration. A key reason is the lack of explainability, which ensures that model predictions are understandable and verifiable by clinicians. Several methods and tools have been proposed to improve explainability, but most reflect developers' perspectives and lack systematic clinical validation. In this work, we gathered insights from radiologist
医疗大模型.abstract: In spite of the strong performance of machine learning (ML) models in radiology, they have not been widely accepted by radiologists, limiting clinical integration. A key reason is the lack of explainability, which ensures that model predictions are understandable and verifiable by clinicians. Several methods and tools have been proposed to improve explainability, but most reflect developers' perspectives and lack systematic clinical validation. In this work, we gathered insights from radiologists with varying experience and specialties into explainable ML requirements through a structured questionnaire. They also highlighted key clinical tasks where ML could be most beneficial and how it might be deployed. Based on their input, we propose guidelines for designing and developing explainable ML models in radiology. These guidelines can help researchers develop clinically useful models, facilitating integration into radiology practice as a supportive tool.
医疗大模型.llm_keywords: 可解释机器学习, 医学影像分析, 放射科医生, 临床验证, 设计指南
医疗大模型.llm_summary: 本文通过问卷调查，探讨了放射科医生对医学影像分析中可解释机器学习模型的期望。研究发现，现有模型因缺乏可解释性而难以被临床接受。基于医生反馈，研究提出了设计和开发可解释机器学习模型的指南，以促进其作为辅助工具融入放射学实践。
医疗大模型.abstract_cn: 尽管机器学习（ML）模型在放射学中表现出色，但并未被放射科医生广泛接受，这限制了其临床整合。一个关键原因是缺乏可解释性，而可解释性确保了临床医生能够理解和验证模型的预测。尽管已经提出了多种方法和工具来提高可解释性，但大多数反映了开发者的视角，缺乏系统的临床验证。在这项工作中，我们通过结构化问卷，收集了来自不同经验和专业的放射科医生对可解释机器学习需求的见解。他们还强调了机器学习可能最有益的关键临床任务及其部署方式。基于他们的输入，我们提出了在放射学中设计和开发可解释机器学习模型的指南。这些指南可以帮助研究人员开发具有临床实用性的模型，促进其作为辅助工具融入放射学实践。

医疗大模型.title: GazeVaLM: A Multi-Observer Eye-Tracking Benchmark for Evaluating Clinical Realism in AI-Generated X-Rays
医疗大模型.summary: We introduce GazeVaLM, a public eye-tracking dataset for studying clinical perception during chest radiograph authenticity assessment. The dataset comprises 960 gaze recordings from 16 expert radiologists interpreting 30 real and 30 synthetic chest X-rays (generated by diffusion based generative AI) under two conditions: diagnostic assessment and real-fake classification (Visual Turing test). For each image-observer pair, we provide raw gaze samples, fixation maps, scanpaths, saliency density ma
医疗大模型.abstract: We introduce GazeVaLM, a public eye-tracking dataset for studying clinical perception during chest radiograph authenticity assessment. The dataset comprises 960 gaze recordings from 16 expert radiologists interpreting 30 real and 30 synthetic chest X-rays (generated by diffusion based generative AI) under two conditions: diagnostic assessment and real-fake classification (Visual Turing test). For each image-observer pair, we provide raw gaze samples, fixation maps, scanpaths, saliency density maps, structured diagnostic labels, and authenticity judgments. We extend the protocol to 6 state-of-the-art multimodal LLMs, releasing their predicted diagnoses, authenticity labels, and confidence scores under matched conditions - enabling direct human-AI comparison at both decision and uncertainty levels. We further provide analyses of gaze agreement, inter-observer consistency, and benchmarking of radiologists versus LLMs in diagnostic accuracy and authenticity detection. GazeVaLM supports research in gaze modeling, clinical decision-making, human-AI comparison, generative image realism assessment, and uncertainty quantification. By jointly releasing visual attention data, clinical labels, and model predictions, we aim to facilitate reproducible research on how experts and AI systems perceive, interpret, and evaluate medical images. The dataset is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/davidcwong/GazeVaLM.
医疗大模型.llm_keywords: 眼动追踪, 人工智能生成X射线, 临床感知, 多模态大语言模型, 人机对比
医疗大模型.llm_summary: 本文介绍了GazeVaLM，一个用于评估AI生成胸部X射线临床真实性的公开眼动追踪数据集。该数据集包含16位放射科专家解读真实与合成X射线时的眼动数据，并扩展至6个多模态大语言模型，以支持人机在诊断和不确定性层面的对比研究。
医疗大模型.abstract_cn: 我们介绍了GazeVaLM，一个用于研究胸部X光片真实性评估过程中临床感知的公开眼动追踪数据集。该数据集包含来自16位放射科专家在两种条件下（诊断评估和真假分类，即视觉图灵测试）解读30张真实和30张合成（由基于扩散的生成式AI生成）胸部X射线时的960条眼动记录。对于每个图像-观察者配对，我们提供了原始注视点样本、注视点图、扫描路径、显著性密度图、结构化诊断标签和真实性判断。我们将该协议扩展到6个最先进的多模态大语言模型，发布了它们在匹配条件下的预测诊断、真实性标签和置信度分数——从而能够在决策和不确定性层面进行直接的人机比较。我们进一步提供了注视一致性、观察者间一致性的分析，以及放射科医生与大语言模型在诊断准确性和真实性检测方面的基准测试。GazeVaLM支持注视建模、临床决策、人机比较、生成图像真实性评估和不确定性量化方面的研究。通过联合发布视觉注意力数据、临床标签和模型预测，我们旨在促进关于专家和AI系统如何感知、解释和评估医学图像的可重复研究。数据集可在 https://huggingface.co/datasets/davidcwong/GazeVaLM 获取。

医疗智能体.title: Towards Autonomous Mechanistic Reasoning in Virtual Cells
医疗智能体.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have recently gained significant attention as a promising approach to accelerate scientific discovery. However, their application in open-ended scientific domains such as biology remains limited, primarily due to the lack of factually grounded and actionable explanations. To address this, we introduce a structured explanation formalism for virtual cells that represents biological reasoning as mechanistic action graphs, enabling systematic verification and falsificati
医疗智能体.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have recently gained significant attention as a promising approach to accelerate scientific discovery. However, their application in open-ended scientific domains such as biology remains limited, primarily due to the lack of factually grounded and actionable explanations. To address this, we introduce a structured explanation formalism for virtual cells that represents biological reasoning as mechanistic action graphs, enabling systematic verification and falsification. Building upon this, we propose VCR-Agent, a multi-agent framework that integrates biologically grounded knowledge retrieval with a verifier-based filtering approach to generate and validate mechanistic reasoning autonomously. Using this framework, we release VC-TRACES dataset, which consists of verified mechanistic explanations derived from the Tahoe-100M atlas. Empirically, we demonstrate that training with these explanations improves factual precision and provides a more effective supervision signal for downstream gene expression prediction. These results underscore the importance of reliable mechanistic reasoning for virtual cells, achieved through the synergy of multi-agent and rigorous verification.
医疗智能体.llm_keywords: 大语言模型, 虚拟细胞, 机制推理, 多智能体框架, 生物知识检索
医疗智能体.llm_summary: 本文针对大语言模型在生物学等开放科学领域应用受限的问题，提出了一种用于虚拟细胞的结构化解释形式，将生物推理表示为机制行动图。基于此，作者提出了VCR-Agent多智能体框架，该框架整合了基于生物知识的检索和基于验证器的过滤方法，以自主生成和验证机制推理。研究还发布了VC-TRACES数据集，并证明使用这些经过验证的解释进行训练能提高事实准确性，并为下游基因表达预测提供更有效的监督信号。
医疗智能体.abstract_cn: 大语言模型（LLMs）作为加速科学发现的一种有前景的方法，最近受到了极大关注。然而，它们在生物学等开放科学领域的应用仍然有限，这主要是由于缺乏基于事实且可操作的解释。为了解决这个问题，我们为虚拟细胞引入了一种结构化的解释形式，将生物推理表示为机制行动图（mechanistic action graphs），从而支持系统性的验证和证伪。在此基础上，我们提出了VCR-Agent，这是一个多智能体框架（multi-agent framework），它整合了基于生物知识的检索（biologically grounded knowledge retrieval）和基于验证器的过滤方法（verifier-based filtering approach），以自主生成和验证机制推理。利用该框架，我们发布了VC-TRACES数据集，该数据集包含从Tahoe-100M图谱中提取的经过验证的机制解释。实验表明，使用这些解释进行训练可以提高事实精确度，并为下游基因表达预测（gene expression prediction）提供更有效的监督信号。这些结果强调了通过多智能体协同和严格验证来实现虚拟细胞可靠机制推理的重要性。
